name: Garden Macaroni Salad
recipeIngredient: 6 Ripe plum tomatoes, each cut into 8 pieces
1 large Yellow pepper, seeded, cut into 1/4 inch dice
3/4 lb Snow peas, lightly blanched, cut lengthwise into 1/4 inch slivers
1/2 lb Tender green beans, lightly blanched, cut into 1-inch lengths
1 medium Zucchini, diced into 1/4 inch pieces
3/4 cup Slivered fresh basil leaves (reserve 2 tbsp)
recipeInstructions:
1. Dressing: In a large bowl, whisk together Dijon mustard, red wine vinegar, garlic, and sugar. Slowly drizzle in olive oil, whisking constantly. Season with salt and pepper, then set aside for at least 1 hour for flavors to blend.
2. Cook pasta: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Cook the pasta until just tender (al dente). Drain, rinse under cold water, and drain again.
3. Combine salad ingredients: Place the cooked pasta in a large bowl and toss with 3/4 cup of the dressing. Add tomatoes, yellow pepper, raisins, and walnuts. Toss pasta and other ingredients together.
4. Add remaining vegetables and herbs: Twenty minutes before serving, add the lightly blanched snow peas, green beans, scallions, zucchini, black olives, and 3/4 cup slivered fresh basil. Toss to combine and adjust seasonings.
5. Finish salad: Just before serving, toss with additional dressing to moisten if desired. Then toss with Parmesan cheese and garnish with reserved 2 tbsp slivered basil leaves.
